summary: Consistent, context-aware ESC behavior across dialogs.

description.text:
The Preferences dialog did not close when pressing ESC if a row in a list/table (e.g., Citation key generator → Key patterns) was selected or being edited.
This created an inconsistent and unintuitive user experience:

ESC worked in some tabs but not others.

When a cell editor was active, the event was consumed by the control before the dialog could handle it.

Users had to manually click outside or use the mouse to close the dialog.

The ESC (close) keybinding was only handled at the Scene level after child controls processed the event (bubbling phase).
When a table/list/tree cell editor was active, the event was consumed and never reached the dialog.

Installed a capturing phase key event filter in PreferencesDialogView after the dialog’s Scene is initialized.

When CLOSE binding (ESC) is pressed:

If any list, table, or tree control is currently editing, the event is left unconsumed — allowing the control to cancel the edit naturally.

If no editing is in progress, the dialog is closed immediately and the event is consumed.

Applied this only to the Preferences dialog scope (localized fix).

Fixes #8888

---

summary: Fix Ctrl+W not closing the current tab

description.text:
Closes #12530 

Remove the Close Library menu item from File menu. Remove the shortcut <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>W</kbd> from tab's context menu and set it in `org.jabref.gui.frame.JabRefFrame#initKeyBindings` instead.

### Steps to test
1. Open at least 2 tabs.
2. Choose randomly a tab, except the first one. Press <kbd>Ctrl</kbd> + <kbd>W</kbd> and the current tab should be closed.
3. Right-click randomly a tab and click Close Library. The tab should be closed regardless whether it is the current tab.

[Short video](https://www.loom.com/share/3091ec42ba6f4cc7a3b61b87471d3521?sid=a7698d3a-94c2-43fa-8cd7-6055cc9ac447) showing step 3.

summary: Modernize ComboBox navigation in Web Search and Preferences (#14083)

description.text: Hello! I'm new to open-source and this is my first PR to JabRef. I've done my best to follow the contribution guidelines, but I appreciate any feedback or suggestions you have. Looking forward to contributing more! Closes #14083 This PR implements improved keyboard navigation and filtering for ComboBoxes in key JabRef panels: WebSearchPaneView.java: Added keyboard jump/cycle for the search ComboBox so users can quickly select services (e.g., typing “L” cycles through “LOBID”). GeneralTab.java: Enabled type-to-jump navigation for the language ComboBox in Preferences. CustomEntryTypesTab.java: Replaced simple entry typing with live autocomplete filtering as users type in the entry field selection ComboBox. No new Checkstyle or unit test failures are introduced with these changes; remaining test failures are unrelated to these edits; Steps to test: In Web Search, click the service ComboBox and use keyboard input to jump and cycle between matching services. In Preferences > General, open the language ComboBox and jump by typing. In Preferences > Entry Types, add or edit a field—the field selection dropdown will now live-filter as you type. - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] I manually tested my changes in running JabRef (always required) - [/] I added JUnit tests for changes (if applicable) - [x] I added screenshots in the PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [/] I described the change in `CHANGELOG.md` in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [/] I checked the [user documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? summary: Prevent custom entry types with spaces

description.text: BibTeX has problems when the entry type contains spaces. JabRef should not allow custom entry types to contain spaces. Adapt the validator: https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/4d2fd15fe244fed17bb6eb31c8dc958f2e4a265c/jabgui/src/main/java/org/jabref/gui/preferences/customentrytypes/CustomEntryTypesTabViewModel.java#L72-L75 Triggered by this post. https://discourse.jabref.org/t/export-list-of-import-errors/3649/10 --- Background: one can customize entry types at

summary: Support combo box in main table (for content selectors)

description.text: 1. Create a new library "demo.bib" 6. Add new entry 7. In "BibTeX source", add `icore = {abc}` ```bibtex @Article{demo, icore = {abc} } ``` 2. Library properties 3. Add "Content selectors" for `ICORE` 3. File > Preferences > Entry table 4. Add custom column "ICORE"\ 5. Save, restart JabRef See at tab "Other fields", there is are the contents displayed However, clicking on the field on the main table does not do anything: There is something done for the viewed field: --- Task: Similar behavior should happen for fields with a content selector. Hints: 1. Possible field values can be read from `databaseContext.getMetaData().getContentSelectorValuesForField(field)` 1. Adapt `org.jabref.gui.maintable.MainTableColumnFactory#createColumn` to handle the case if the are content selector values and display them 2. `org.jabref.gui.maintable.columns.ContentSelectorColumn` similar to `org.jabref.gui.maintable.columns.SpecialFieldColumn#SpecialFieldColumn` (but more easy - just use OptionalValueTableCellFactory`

summary: Feature: Move group sort actions to a submenu

description.text: Closes #14017 This change improves the user interface by moving the four "Sort subgroups..." actions from the main group context menu into their own dedicated "Sort subgroups..." submenu. This makes the menu cleaner and more organized for users. ### Steps to test 1. Run the JabRef application. 2. In the "Groups" panel on the left, create a parent group (e.g., "My Research"). 3. Select the parent group and then create at least one subgroup (e.g., "Papers"). 4. Right-click on the parent group ("My Research"). 5. Confirm that you see a new "Sort subgroups..." menu item. 6. Hover your mouse over it and confirm that the four sorting options (A-Z, Z-A, etc.) appear in a fly-out submenu. ### Mandatory checks - [x] I own the copyright of the code submitted and I license it under the [MIT license](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/main/LICENSE) - [x] I manually tested my changes in running JabRef (always required) - [/] I added JUnit tests for changes (if applicable) - [x] I added screenshots in the PR description (if change is visible to the user) - [x] I described the change in `CHANGELOG.md` in a way that is understandable for the average user (if change is visible to the user) - [/] I checked the [user documentation](https://docs.jabref.org/): Is the information available and up to date? summary: Fix search query reset issue after entry deletion

description.text:
Closes #13984

This PR fixes the issue where search results would show stale entries after deleting entries from search results, making it appear as if the search query was reset even though it remained in the search bar.

Added an `EntriesRemovedEvent` listener to `MainTableDataModel.SearchIndexListener` that refreshes search matches when entries are removed from the database, ensuring the filtered list is properly updated to prevent stale entries and maintain the search query state during entry manipulation operations.

### Steps to test

1. Open JabRef and load a library with multiple entries
2. Perform a search using the search bar (e.g., search for a specific author or keyword)
3. Verify that search results are displayed correctly
4. Select one or more entries from the search results
5. Delete the selected entries (right-click → Delete or press Delete key)
6. Verify that:
   - The search query remains in the search bar
   - Only remaining matching entries are displayed
   - No stale entries appear in the table
   - The search state is properly maintained
